# UC-22.15.36 · PLC Local Console Login Without Corporate Identity

## Description

Finds controller-local or maintenance port logins that cannot be mapped to a named human, weakening component-level identification.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for IEC 62443 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes ot_eng and sourcetypes plc:access. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Enable user management on intelligent devices; (2) disable default maintenance accounts; (3) physical locks on USB; (4) quarterly audit export. Indexes required: `index=ot_eng`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=plc:access`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Rockwell/Siemens vendor forwarders → `index=ot_eng`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="plc:access"` or vendor-specific.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Enable user management on intelligent devices; (2) disable default maintenance accounts; (3) physical locks on USB; (4) quarterly audit export.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot_eng sourcetype="plc:access" earliest=-30d
| eval user_id=coalesce(user, operator_slot)
| where user_id IN ("0","anonymous","MAINT","") OR match(user_id, "^\*+$")
| stats count by controller, user_id, src_interface
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**PLC Local Console Login Without Corporate Identity (IEC 62443-4-2 / CR 1.1)** — Finds controller-local or maintenance port logins that cannot be mapped to a named human, weakening component-level identification.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="plc:access"` or vendor-specific. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Rockwell/Siemens vendor forwarders → `index=ot_eng`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot_eng; **sourcetype**: plc:access. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ot_eng, sourcetype="plc:access", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **user_id** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where user_id IN ("0","anonymous","MAINT","") OR match(user_id, "^\*+$")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by controller, user_id, src_interface** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the OT engineering record and session recording the control owner would use in an investigation. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table, Bar, Single value (anonymous logins).

## SPL

```spl
index=ot_eng sourcetype="plc:access" earliest=-30d
| eval user_id=coalesce(user, operator_slot)
| where user_id IN ("0","anonymous","MAINT","") OR match(user_id, "^\*+$")
| stats count by controller, user_id, src_interface
| sort - count
```

## Visualization

Table, Bar, Single value (anonymous logins). Key fields: `controller`, `src_interface`, `user_id`.
